title: Adopt concise-by-default MCP tool responses with a response_format toggle
source: Anthropic engineering: 'Writing effective tools for agents' — token-efficient responses, pagination, concise vs detailed (knowledge cutoff Jan 2026; live web search unavailable this run)
description: MCP tools like board/list_tickets/project_status likely return full JSON blobs that consume large context when Rick calls them repeatedly during a sprint. Anthropic's tool-design guidance recommends returning concise human-meaningful summaries by default, supporting pagination/filtering, and offering a detail level enum. This cuts token cost per orchestration loop and reduces context bloat that degrades long sessions.
proposed_changes: Add a `response_format: Literal['concise','detailed']='concise'` param to list_tickets, board, and project_status in mcp_server.py. Concise returns id/title/status/assignee only; detailed returns full objects. Add `limit`/`offset` (or status filter) to list_tickets to paginate large ticket sets. Define the shared response shapes in schemas.py so SwiftUI/roro consumers stay in sync. Default callers in orchestrate.py to concise.
